more.title: How the Skeleton of Mutapa Times is Based on Sarah Charlesworth's Work
more.description: Conceptual artist Sarah Charlesworth's 1979 study of how newspapers covered a solar eclipse inspired the framework behind The Mutapa Times.

more.title: Silicon battlefields: Why Big Tech is a target in the US-Israeli war on Iran
more.description: IN traditional wars, armies directed their firepower toward visible strategic assets – military bases, weapons factories, airfields – where supply lines could be mapped and battle plans drawn with relative certainty. more.description: Twenty‐six years ago, the late Iraqi president Saddam Hussein made a decision that resonated far beyond Baghdad. more.description: Source: Serial armed robbers nabbed –Newsday Zimbabwe BULAWAYO, Mar. 25 (NewsDay Live) – Police in Masvingo have confirmed the arrest of two serial armed robbers who had been terrorising Kwekwe residents, targeting network service providers’ base stations. more.title: Rewiring Zimbabwe’s Financial Future: Why Data, Infrastructure and Technology Are Now the Real Currency of Power
more.description: IN Zimbabwe, conversations about economic recovery have long been framed around currency stability, debt restructuring, and commodity exports. Yet beneath these familiar debates, a quieter but far more consequential transformation is underway—one that will determine whether the country merely survives or genuinely reinvents itself.
